environment.database: PostgreSQL (local dev DB; bridge tests are SimpleTestCase, no DB)

commands: backend: python manage.py test execution.tests_bridge_symbols execution.tests_e2b_shadow
current_bridge_symbol_gate: REPLACED: scripts/mt5_signal_bridge.py ALLOWED_SYMBOLS (was line 79) — static allowlist used by validate_job_safety (strategy PLACE_ORDER pre-check).

REPLACED: scripts/mt5_signal_bridge.py DEMO_ORDER_ALLOWED_SYMBOLS (was line 84) — static allowlist used by execute_demo_order (POST /mt5/order, the Wayond demo path) AND shadow_order_check (POST /mt5/order_check).
NOW MT5-native: validate_broker_symbol(mt5, symbol) — symbol_info() then symbol_select() if not visible; fail-closed SYMBOL_NOT_AVAILABLE_ON_MT5 / SYMBOL_NOT_SELECTABLE_ON_MT5.

PRESERVED (unchanged rails): MAX_LOT_SIZE/DEMO_ORDER_MAX_LOT_SIZE (lot cap), ALLOWED_SIDES/DEMO_ORDER_ALLOWED_SIDES (side), SL/TP mandatory in validate_job_safety, demo-only (account trade_mode==0), order_check (shadow path).
OUT OF SCOPE (separate/legacy bridge, not in the Wayond execution path): scripts/mt5_demo_bridge.py — its own DEMO_ORDER constants left unchanged.
expected_results: The bridge's hardcoded symbol allowlist is replaced by MT5-native validation at all three symbol gates (validate_job_safety/execute_mt5_trade strategy path, execute_demo_order live demo path, shadow_order_check dry-run path).

payload['symbol'] (the broker symbol resolved by the backend registry) is validated with mt5.symbol_info; if missing -> SYMBOL_NOT_AVAILABLE_ON_MT5; if present-but-not-visible -> mt5.symbol_select, and if that fails -> SYMBOL_NOT_SELECTABLE_ON_MT5; else proceed to the existing order_check/order_send logic.
EURUSD/XAUUSD still trade; BTCUSD (and any broker-supported/suffixed symbol) trades iff mt5.symbol_info offers it; unsupported/non-selectable symbols fail closed with NO order and NO order_check.

The broker symbol is what is placed (request['symbol']); provider_symbol (Wayond symbol) is preserved in logs/result if present and is NOT added to the MT5 request. The worker now forwards provider_symbol to the bridge if present.
Lot cap, side, SL/TP mandatory, demo-only and order_check rails are unchanged; full backend suite green; no migration.
actual_results: execution.tests_bridge_symbols + execution.tests_e2b_shadow: Ran 52 tests OK. New tests cover: validate_broker_symbol (available / exact-suffix / not-available / non-visible-selectable / non-visible-non-selectable); execute_demo_order (1 EURUSD, 2 XAUUSD accepted; 3 BTCUSD accepted when available; 4 broker suffix placed under broker symbol + provider preserved + not in MT5 request; 5+11 unsupported rejected no order_send; 6 non-visible selectable accepted; 7 non-visible non-selectable rejected; 9 lot cap; side gate; 12 provider preserved on reject; non-demo refused); shadow_order_check (10 order_check still required; BTCUSD shadow accepted; unsupported -> no order_check; provider preserved + request identical to live); strategy path (8 SL/TP mandatory; non-baseline no longer blocked at pre-check; blank rejected; lot cap; execute_mt5_trade rejects unavailable no order_send); boundary (no ALLOWED_SYMBOLS/DEMO_ORDER_ALLOWED_SYMBOLS constants or source references remain).

Updated the one backend test that asserted the OLD allowlist: tests_e2b_shadow.test_invalid_symbol_fails_before_mt5 -> test_unsupported_symbol_rejected_by_mt5_no_order (BTCUSD now MT5-validated: symbol_info None -> SYMBOL_NOT_AVAILABLE_ON_MT5, no order_check/order_send).
Full backend suite: Ran 536 tests OK (was 509; +27 new/updated; no regressions).

py_compile: bridge + worker compile clean. makemigrations --check: no change from this packet (the only drift reported is a pre-existing strategies index-rename, unrelated — this packet touches no models).
make governance-check: EVIDENCE-LINT PASS; secret-scan clean.
Adversarial multi-lens review + synthesis: see reviewer field / PR.
limitations: Repo-only. NOT deployed to Windows/prod. No order_send, no arming, no risk-cap change. The currently-armed demo runs on the DEPLOYED (previous) bridge and is unaffected by this branch.

The bridge (scripts/mt5_signal_bridge.py) and worker (backend/mt5_trade_ingest_worker.py) are deployed MANUALLY to the Windows MT5 box / prod host (the worker script is volume-mounted; the bridge runs on Windows). Merging changes neither running process. End-to-end non-baseline (e.g. BTCUSD) execution needs: (a) this bridge deployed to the Windows box, (b) the worker script updated on the host, and (c) a backend BrokerInstrument sync + the backend registry code deployed (GFX-PKT-BROKER-SYMBOL-REGISTRY, also repo-only).

The bridge validates the EXACT symbol it receives; symbol RESOLUTION (provider->broker, suffix mapping, ambiguity) is owned by the backend registry. The bridge does not itself enumerate broker symbols or resolve suffixes.

execute_demo_order treats SL/TP as OPTIONAL (unchanged behaviour — SL/TP mandatoriness is enforced upstream by the backend plan/_validate and, for the strategy path, by validate_job_safety). This packet did not add SL/TP enforcement to the demo endpoint; it preserved existing behaviour.
Bridge tests run as Django SimpleTestCase that load the standalone script via importlib and fake MetaTrader5 via sys.modules; they do not exercise a real MT5 terminal.
